context: PR #42 has one must-fix review comment on lib/db/queries.ts:45. A subagent runs the reproduction loop: it writes a failing test, runs it against current code (RED captured), applies the proposed FIX_OLD_STRING/FIX_NEW_STRING, and re-runs — but the post-fix run still fails (`npx tsc --noEmit` exits non-zero with a TS2345 error on the changed line, so GREEN is never reached).
expected_behavior: The subagent reverts the Edit by swapping old_string/new_string AND deletes the repro test it created, then confirms `git diff --stat lib/db/queries.ts` and `git status --porcelain` show the tree exactly as found

Because RED was reached but GREEN was not, the fix is NOT EVIDENCE_TIER: reproduced — the subagent reports the captured RED_OUTPUT and the failing post-fix output, sets EVIDENCE_TIER accordingly (unverified for the proposed fix, since it does not resolve the reproduction), and downgrades CONFIDENCE by 1
Step 5 surfaces this finding under the 'Unconfirmed — reasoning only' heading (or with a ✗ evidence marker), never with a ✓, and excludes it from auto-apply
Step 6 shows the user the proposed diff alongside the RED reproduction and the failing post-fix command output ('$ npx tsc --noEmit → 2 (type errors)' and the TS2345 snippet) so they can decide

Auto-apply mode (if selected at Step 4) MUST NOT auto-apply this fix: only fixes with confidence>=4 AND EVIDENCE_TIER in {reproduced, tooling-confirmed} auto-apply; anything fix-validated/unverified/failed/dirty-cleanup-needed always goes to manual review regardless of confidence

If the user picks 'Apply fix' anyway, Step 10 reruns the full validation suite and triggers the 'Full validation failed after applying fixes' AskUserQuestion with Revert last fix / Revert all fixes / Commit anyway / Show me the failure options
failure_modes: Subagent fails to revert the Edit or delete the repro test after validation and leaves the working tree dirty without reporting EVIDENCE_TIER: dirty-cleanup-needed
Auto-apply mode applies the fix because its CONFIDENCE is still >=4, ignoring the evidence-tier gate
Presents the fix as 'reproduced' or '✓ verified' despite GREEN never being reached
Reasons that 'the bug is clearly real' and marks it confirmed without an actual RED→GREEN transition
